summary: Socioeconomic status influences access to healthcare resources during early motherhood, which may indirectly affect a woman’s ability to receive or understand educational materials about parenting and maternal wellness. However, no studies directly examine differences in specific learning needs based on income level; thus, while plausible, this relationship remains speculative due to insufficient empirical data linking SES to distinct learning demands.
